title: MLB writer predicts Roman Anthony will win AL MVP this season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony predicted to win 2026 AL MVP by The Athletic's Eno Sarris.
Anthony's rookie season with the Red Sox ended with a .292 average, eight home runs, and 32 RBI.
He batted .280 with two home runs for Team USA in the 2026 World Baseball Classic.

title: Pedestrian killed in Roxbury hit-and-run
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A pedestrian was killed in a hit-and-run incident Friday morning at Southampton and Bradston in Roxbury.
The pedestrian was pronounced dead at a local hospital after the incident, according to Boston police.
The vehicle was found, but the driver has not been arrested; the investigation is ongoing.

title: Here’s what meteorologists are saying about chances for snow through Monday
author: Jack Pickell
bullets: National Weather Service expects wet snow accumulation of up to 2 inches near I-90 by Monday.
Rain changes to wet snow early Monday from northern Massachusetts to Connecticut and Rhode Island.
Meteorologists forecast snow showers Monday night, with potential coating on grassy surfaces and hills.

title: Mass. police officer arrested for OUI, department says
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Norwood police officer Shawn Willman was arrested for operating under the influence of alcohol while off duty.
Officer Willman has been placed on administrative leave pending the outcome of the criminal case and department investigations.
No court information regarding the case has been filed as of Sunday evening, according to the police department.

title: Police: Mass. man allegedly stabbed friend during fight over woman at Miami Beach bachelor party
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Olajuwon Shawn Dickerson, 32, of Marston Mills, was arrested in Miami Beach for allegedly stabbing his friend at a bachelor party.
The incident occurred after an argument between Dickerson and his friend, Brian Gordon, over women they met at bars.
Dickerson is charged with aggravated battery and held without bond, while both men were treated for their injuries.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-1 loss to St. Louis City SC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: New England Revolution lost 3-1 to St. Louis City SC, marking their third defeat in four games this season.
St. Louis secured victory with goals from Simon Becher and Chris Durkin, despite an early lead by New England's Ilay Feingold.
Revolution forward Leo Campana exited with an injury; coach Mitrović said it is unrelated to his previous injury.

title: Don’t be fooled by this fake court notice scam in Mass., NH, authorities warn
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Authorities in Massachusetts and New Hampshire warn of fake court notices demanding payment for traffic violations.
Scam notices contain errors like 'State of Massachuseetts' and generic names, and instruct victims to scan a QR code.
Recipients advised to ignore messages, avoid scanning QR codes, and verify through official court contact details.

title: The trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons begins this week. Here’s what to know.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: North Andover officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons's trial begins March 23 for assault with a dangerous weapon, following last summer's shooting.
The shooting occurred during service of a restraining order sought by Fitzsimmons’s ex-fiancé, with contradictory accounts around the incident.
Fitzsimmons waived her right to a jury trial; the bench trial will be conducted by Judge Jeffrey Karp in Essex Superior Court.

title: She’s running Boston for patients ‘rebuilding their lives step by step’
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Lina Sinisterra Mazariegos, 44, from Cali, Colombia, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
She began running as a form of self-understanding and sees marathons as lessons in patience, humility, and trust.
Sinisterra Mazariegos dedicates her run to patients at Spaulding, emphasizing recovery as a slow, repetitive, deeply human process.

title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at the House of Blues in Boston, exhibiting energetic performances reminiscent of their early years.
The band maintained tight musical cohesion, despite flamboyant stage antics, highlighting new songs alongside classics.
Opening act The Chats delivered a raw, garage rock performance, setting the mood for The Hives' headlining act.
